title: marketing — model relationships
description: Four owned entities. MarketingEvent is the aggregate root; MarketingDelivery + MarketingDeadLetter attach to it. MarketingProviderConfig is standalone (tenant-scoped) — deliveries reference it via provider slug + provider_config_id. Every outbound FK stays same-tenant per tenancy-columns.md §5. NO application_id / region_id / organization_id / scope_node_id on any owned row.

internal.description: Per-provider-per-attempt delivery log. RESTRICT on event hard-delete via observer.
RESTRICT — deliveries anchor to their event for auditing. An event's hard-delete cascades only after 7-year retention closes.
Dead-letter rows for max-attempts-exceeded events. Zero-or-more per event (one per failed provider).

RESTRICT — dead letters anchor to their event. Financial retention: dead letters retained indefinitely; events retained 7 years. Dead letters may OUTLIVE their events (in which case the FK resolution returns NULL on the event side).
RESTRICT — deliveries hold provider_config_id for auditing the exact credentials used. Config soft-delete does NOT cascade — deliveries preserve the historical config reference.

outbound.description: Every marketing event is tenant-scoped per tenancy-columns.md §3.

Polymorphic subject: User, Athlete, Membership, Subscription, Lead. Each subject_type has its own tenant scoping; MarketingEventObserver validates that subject.tenant_id == event.tenant_id (defense-in-depth against cross-tenant FK smuggling).
Denormalised tenant_id — cascades through event.tenant_id but explicit for query-plan + FK integrity.
The actor who marked the dead-letter resolved. RESTRICT for audit-integrity — user hard-delete cannot orphan resolution records.

inbound.description: Marketing does NOT have inbound relations. No downstream module attaches to marketing rows. Marketing is a leaf in the growth tier — its role is to fan out to external providers, not to be consumed by other domain rows.
inbound.note: The referrals module (Wave 5 sibling) emits its own MarketingEvent rows via the domain-event listener path but does NOT hold an FK on marketing rows. Referrals owns its own tables (`referrals`, `referral_rewards`).

morphs.description: Polymorphic subject of the marketing event. Each supported type is tenant-scoped in its own module. The observer validates cross-model tenant integrity on write.

notes: None of the four owned rows carry `application_id` — cascades through tenant_id → tenants.application_id per tenancy-columns.md §2. Only the 8 named rows carry application_id directly; marketing_* is not among them.
None of the four owned rows carry `region_id`, `organization_id`, or `scope_node_id`. Marketing is a domain-plane concern — the growth/scope substrate does not apply.

MarketingDelivery.provider_config_id is a snapshot reference — when the config is soft-deleted, the delivery still resolves the config via the RESTRICT FK. When the config is hard-purged after the 90-day grace, the FK resolution returns the stale row from the audit trail.

The morph on MarketingEvent.subject is deliberately loose — the module's mapping registry (DomainEventMapper) knows about specific types, but the base schema allows any (subject_type, subject_id) pair. This lets new subject types land without a schema change.

MarketingProviderConfig has NO inverse relation from Delivery marked as hasMany — that's intentional. Deliveries are per-attempt and would double-count if we exposed the inverse. Query deliveries by (tenant, provider) instead.
